title: Iran fires at least 10 ballistic missiles at Ramat David Airbase — first direct Iran-to-Israel attack since April 8 ceasefire (60 days); all intercepted
snippet: IRGC fired at least 10 ballistic missiles at Ramat David Airbase in northern Israel, in retaliation for the Beirut Dahieh strike. This is the first direct Iran-to-Israel missile attack since the April 8 ceasefire — a gap of exactly 60 days. Israeli air defenses intercepted all missiles from at least 3 separate barrages. IRGC statement said it targeted 'the source of acts of aggression' against Lebanese civilians. Iran warned 'any escalation would lead to broader response encompassing all US and Israeli targets.' Covered by NPR, Euronews, CNN, PBS, Axios, Haaretz, Fox News, RedState.
tag_reason: First direct Iran-to-Israel missile attack since April 8 ceasefire — 60-day gap broken; entirely new escalation event

entities.name: Iran Missile Attack on Ramat David (Jun 8)
